invariant.title: INVARIANT — Alaska E. González
invariant.description: Decentralised forensic infrastructure suite for Ring-0 hardware auditing. Air-gapped, Ed25519-signed, bare-metal Arch Linux probe.
invariant.og_description: Go · Rust/Tauri · SvelteKit · Python/Arch. Zero-Trust, air-gapped, Ed25519 cryptography.

invariant.title: INVARIANT
invariant.description: Decentralised forensic suite for Ring-0 hardware auditing. Four cryptographically coupled pillars: Go Control Plane with Ed25519 signing, Rust/Tauri flasher using O_SYNC, SvelteKit edge reverse-proxy, and a Python bare-metal probe running on air-gapped Arch Linux.

header.title: INVARIANT
header.subtitle: Decentralised forensic infrastructure suite designed to intercept and evaluate computational hardware operating at privileged execution rings, sustained by four cryptographically interconnected pillars
header.author: Alaska Elaina González

abstract.title: Abstract
abstract.body: INVARIANT operates as a decentralised forensic infrastructure suite designed to intercept and evaluate computational hardware operating at privileged execution rings (Ring-0). The system rests on four highly decoupled yet cryptographically interconnected pillars: a Control Plane built in Go (invariant-api) handling state management and asymmetric credential issuance; a high-performance SvelteKit interface for operational control (invariant-web); an asynchronous Rust/Tauri engine for NAND memory interaction (invariant_flasher); and a live, self-contained Python/Arch Linux forensic probe for deterministic bare-metal extraction (prob-tex).

s1.heading: 1. Motivation and Context
s1.p1: Modern hardware audits suffer from a systemic blind spot when relying on the host Operating System (Windows/macOS). A user-space system inherently lies: WMI returns cached metrics, drivers collapse under stress, and damaged sectors are abstracted away by OS heuristics. The motivation behind INVARIANT is to establish a Zero-Trust validation mechanism that rejects all mediation from commercial operating systems.
s1.p2: By directly seizing ownership of hardware resources (ACPI, MSR, PCIe, NVMe), it becomes possible to distil the underlying silicon reality. This approach turns a USB drive into a destructive probe of illusions, providing a relentless interface for hardware reliability engineering.

s2.heading: 2. Pipeline Architecture
s2.p1: INVARIANT's orchestration forms an uninterrupted closed loop. The flow begins at the Control Plane, where invariant-api exposes asset telemetry and low-latency S3 pre-signed tokens through invariant-web's Edge reverse proxy. The frontend provides state visibility while the operator downloads and launches invariant_flasher. This program compiles an on-demand ISO generated by forge.sh, destroying partitions on physical media and burning the local boot image together with an asymmetrically signed licence seed. Finally, the prob-tex probe boots from the Air-Gapped environment, executing entirely outside the radar and constraints of host systems.
s2.probtex_p1: Python engine on immutable Arch Linux. Performs block-level introspection by invoking kernel calls (lsblk) to map the bus (NVMe/SATA) while ignoring logical volumes, ensuring raw and sequential I/O without host OS interference.
s2.api_p1: Constructs a canonical JSON payload and signs it with an Ed25519 elliptic-curve key over raw UTF-8 bytes, guaranteeing that licensing data is immutable and verifiable in a fully offline (air-gapped) environment.
s2.web_p1: Hijacks the network topology by operating a server-side reverse proxy (Cloudflare Workers) that mutates and masks requests, bypassing CORS asymmetries through a strict single-origin bridge for the HTTPOnly session.
s2.flasher_p1: Acquires a raw file descriptor directly to the device silicon by injecting the O_SYNC kernel flag, short-circuiting in-memory cache heuristics to guarantee that the ISO overwrites NAND transistors synchronously.

s3.heading: 3. Cryptography and Access Control
s3.p1: The decoupling between cloud and the isolated device demands robust cryptography to mitigate impersonation attacks. Hardware authorisation is encapsulated locally without requiring internet access. The central entity (invariant-api) employs crypto/ed25519 to sign a LicensePayload with deterministic parameters injected into the JSON structure, anchoring absolute expiry dates (Time-Trap) that depend on the HWID generated from the SHA-256 hash of the host's DMI identifiers.
s3.p2: This tokenisation includes a synchronous kill-switch: if the asymmetric proof of the token is rejected, prob-tex immediately triggers a virtual kernel-panic, permanently blocking the forensic report and preventing any data exfiltration in a hostile environment.

s4.heading: 4. Low-Level Evasion and Flashing
s4.p1: Deploying a live OS while evading hostile restrictions requires manipulating the local partition table while bypassing buffers. The invariant_flasher module is built in Rust and employs native OS bindings to force an exclusive open (O_SYNC on POSIX and FILE_FLAG_WRITE_THROUGH on Win32) of the block descriptor /dev/sdX or \\.\PhysicalDriveX.
s4.p2: The subsystem heuristically destroys the MBR, primary GPT, and even the Backup GPT located at binary level in the last LBA sectors (17,408 bytes), eliminating potential mount collisions or 'resurrections' guided by tools such as parted or gdisk, before sequentially injecting the Arch Linux boot block.

s5.heading: 5. The Bare-Metal Forensic Engine
s5.p1: The core of truth resides in prob-tex: a subsystem that runs multiple threads under a strict ThreadPoolExecutor, capable of bypassing xHCI bus delays for USB and SATA/NVMe latencies by iterating block nodes directly (lsblk -J). Raw metadata is extracted by invoking fio, smartctl, and parallel stress-ng spikes on RAM/Motherboard, with sequential runs (due to bus constraints) on CPU/GPU.
s5.p2: The computed entropy index cannot be injected or modified by the hostile ACPI interface; it is protected by sanitising LaTeX strings from BIOS DMI blocks (preventing LaTeX injection attacks), finally dumping an immutable PDF document by invoking the off-grid tectonic rendering binary through ISO-level pre-loaded caches.

s6.heading: 6. Internal Ecosystem Audit
s6.friction_p: The primary stress zone falls on the synchronous I/O bottleneck during media writing (flashing) and hardware enumeration. Abstractions such as udevadm settle on Linux, or the FILE_FLAG_WRITE_THROUGH streams of Windows in the flasher, impose severe timeouts. If the xHCI bus or the physical device fails to synchronise asynchronously with kernel descriptors, silent failures or race conditions arise that collapse the entire pipe, forcing the flasher to apply static waits as a defensive tactic.
s6.zerotrust_p: The ecosystem's design assumes a threat vector where no endpoint is trusted. The host OS is considered deeply compromised and is therefore ignored; the system injects its own bare-metal environment based on immutable Linux (prob-tex) to evaluate thermal and wear telemetry. Web client assertions are never trusted, with subscription expiries and grace periods revalidated through session interception in the backend (user_assets / hardware_trials). BIOS and reported hardware are treated as potentially malicious inputs, with rigorous LaTeX injection sanitisation applied in prob-tex's main.py when reading DMI fields.

s7.heading: 7. Operational Considerations
s7.p1: INVARIANT requires tolerance for and mitigation design against stochastic local hardware failures, such as unstable drives or decaying USB controllers. To this end, strict fallback mechanisms are implemented (e.g. falling back to /dev/nvme0n1 subsystems if PCIe detection collapses) and silent firstboot locks through aggressive masking (systemd-firstboot suppression at initramfs level).
s7.p2: This ecosystem turns a USB drive into a destructive probe of illusions, providing a relentless interface for hardware reliability engineering and ensuring deterministic integrity against underlying silicon asymmetries.
